description:
## Goal
Introduce the vocabulary for "this synthesized pattern-bind fail edge is unreachable" as a first-class outcome. Producer: `ir_typer` (it already computes the proof to emit `type/unreachable-arm`). No codegen changes in this ticket — the consumer comes in T2.

## Background
Today `Expr::Match(pat, rhs)` lowers to `lower_pattern_bind` which always synthesizes a fail block with `Halt(match_error)` (`src/ir_lower.rs:1859`). When the inferred scrutinee type makes that fail edge dead, the typer emits `type/unreachable-arm`. With warnings-as-errors, every irrefutable destructure is unshippable.

Recent commits (`fz-9pr`, `fz-f88`) established the callsite-outcomes pattern: data-model + producer + reader, with diagnostics as readers. This ticket extends that vocabulary.

## Scope
- Add an outcome variant naming "exhaustive match arm" (or similar — pick the spelling that fits the existing lattice).
- Tag the synthesized arm at `ir_lower` time so producers/consumers can identify it (vs. user-written `case`/`with` arms).
- `ir_typer` publishes the outcome when narrowing proves the fail edge has scrutinee type `none`.
- Suppress `type/unreachable-arm` for synthesized arms (the outcome is the real signal; the warning was a stand-in).
- Optional dump-side surface so the outcome is inspectable in `expected.outcomes` snapshots.

## Out of scope (later tickets)
- T2: a reducer pass actually elides the fail block based on this outcome.
- T3: interp Discriminant for list-cons.

## BEAM-conform invariant to preserve
Refutable binds (e.g. `[h|t] = e` where `e` may be `[]`) MUST keep the fail block and produce a runtime `match_error`. The outcome only fires when the typer can prove exhaustivity.

## Done when
- New outcome variant exists and is published by `ir_typer` for synthesized arms with provably-dead fail edges.
- `type/unreachable-arm` no longer fires on synthesized `Expr::Match` arms.
- Existing fixtures unchanged in their `expected.txt`; relevant ones gain `expected.outcomes` lines for the new outcome.
- All tests pass.
title: T1: outcome variant + ir_typer producer for exhaustive match arms
